summary: Small single-storey rectangular brick building with a raised frame/porch around it. Exterior shows a lower brick wall band, a white smooth band above, a dark narrow cornice, and a flat dark roof inset. Windows run as a horizontal series along the long sides. Stone corner pillars and a low white outer border/ledge surround the building on ground level.

shape.overall_form: simple rectangular box with an inner hollow/roof inset
shape.symmetry: bilaterally symmetric along long axis (appears symmetric front-to-back and side-to-side in plan)

materials.name: quartz (or smooth white)
materials.role: upper wall band and outer border/ledge

materials.name: wood_planks
materials.role: narrow base strip between ground and brick (likely oak planks)

elements: continuous horizontal windows (series of glass panes set in a row)
entrance: small recessed opening visible on one long side (uncertain internal door type)
flat roof with raised outer coping made of stone-like slabs
raised white border/ledge around building at ground level
wooden planks skirting at base of brick
rebuild_hints: Build a rectangular foundation ~9x17 blocks (count visually from top-down images); place a one-block-wide white border around the perimeter raised one block from surrounding ground.

Raise walls: start with a wooden plank base (1 block high), then 2–3 blocks of brick for the lower façade with regularly spaced glass pane windows centered on the long sides, add a continuous white (quartz/smooth) band one block high above the brick, then a one-block-high dark nether_brick cornice around the top.
Corners: place stonebrick/stone-like pillar blocks at each external corner spanning full wall height.
Roof: inset flat dark roof (nether_brick) recessed one block from the cornice; surround the roof edge with stone slab coping (slab_stone).
Windows: use glass panes placed in a continuous row at the same vertical level across the long sides; leave a small recessed entrance opening on one long side aligned with the white border/ledge.
uncertainties: Exact block counts for width/depth are estimated from images; actual counts may differ by ±1–2 blocks.
Material identification for white smooth blocks assumed quartz/smooth stone; could be other white block (e.g., smooth sandstone).
Middle dark material identified as nether_brick for both cornice and roof interior; roof interior texture might be a different dark block.
Wood type for narrow base strip is estimated (looks like oak planks) but could be a different wood variant.
Interior layout, door type, and any interior partitions are not visible and therefore unknown.
